control_map_artifact.visual_assessment: Diagnostic preserves the same high-contrast facial, hair, collar, and blazer contour information as the inspected v1 lineart input.

qa_findings: Generated image remains a coherent hyperrealistic head-and-shoulders portrait with clean eye alignment, believable hair, stable face proportions, and realistic blazer/shirt clothing.
Compared with v1, the background improved from stylized vertical light strips to a more natural windowed interior, directly addressing one QA note.
Skin texture is slightly more natural than v1, with visible soft cheek/forehead texture, but still retains a mild polished editorial look.
Lineart conditioning did not leak visible ink outlines or black contour stains into the face, hair, collar, or clothing.
Hands, contact points, and full-body anatomy are not visible and are not certified by this sample.
remaining_risks: Single local follow-up image is not full lineart lane certification.
Hands, full-body anatomy, contact points, and multi-seed robustness are not certified because they are not visible or not sampled.
Target-runtime EC2 object_info/path/hash/input proof, bounded generation, pullback, technical QA, and strict whole-image visual QA remain separate gates.
next_action: Keep v2 as the preferred local lineart prompt candidate if continuing lineart; otherwise continue local-first with OpenPose/DWPose or normal ControlNet model proof.
